steps.prompt: Which word is the verb of the relative clause?

hints.perdet: 'Perdet' is the main clause verb, not the relative clause verb
hints.facere: 'Facere' is an infinitive meaning 'to make' — it complements 'quaesierit'
hints.Quicumque: 'Quicumque' is an indefinite relative pronoun meaning 'whoever'

hints.to ask: 'To ask' is 'rogare' or 'petere' — 'quaesierit' comes from 'quaerere' (to seek)

hints.perfect subjunctive: The form is identical — but with future 'perdet' in the apodosis, future perfect is appropriate
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect indicative would be 'quaesivit' — 'quaesierit' has the -eri- marker
hints.pluperfect subjunctive: Pluperfect subjunctive would be 'quaesivisset' — 'quaesierit' is future perfect

hints.queror, queri, questus sum, —: These are the parts of 'queri' (to complain) — a deponent
hints.quiesco, quiescere, quievi, quietum: These are the parts of 'quiescere' (to rest)
hints.rogo, rogare, rogavi, rogatum: These are the parts of 'rogare' (to ask)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'perdit' — 'perdet' has the future -e- vowel (3rd conj.)
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'perdat' — 'perdet' is future indicative

hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the parts of 'dare' (to give) — the simple verb
hints.perficio, perficere, perfeci, perfectum: These are the parts of 'perficere' (to accomplish)
hints.perfero, perferre, pertuli, perlatum: These are the parts of 'perferre' (to endure)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'anima' — the -am ending marks accusative
Nominative fem. would be 'salva' — 'salvam' agrees with 'animam' in the accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative fem. would be 'salva' — 'salvam' is accusative
hints.adverb: 'Salvam' is an adjective, not an adverb — it modifies 'animam'

steps.prompt: What type of conditional sentence is this?

hints.present general (present + present): Present general uses present tense — this has future perfect and future
hints.contrary to fact (pluperfect subjunctive: Contrary to fact uses subjunctive — 'perdet' is future indicative
hints.mixed condition: Mixed conditions combine different types — this is straightforwardly future more vivid

translation: Whoever will have sought to save his life will lose it
